title: Patient · gender & birthsex
notes:
Branch dimension: how the Patient__person edge derives `gender_concept_id`, `gender_source_value`, and `gender_source_concept_id` from `Patient.gender` (administrative-gender) and the US Core `us-core-birthsex` extension.

Key mechanics, grounded in mapspec/etl/Patient__person.sql + cm.gender_to_omop (the materialized two-group ConceptMap mapspec/profiles/gender-to-omop.cm.json):

  gender_concept_id        = COALESCE(g.concept_id, 0)
  gender_source_value      = COALESCE(us_core_birthsex, gender)
  gender_source_concept_id = COALESCE(g.source_concept_id, 0)
  where g = cm.gender_to_omop  ON  source_code = COALESCE(us_core_birthsex, gender)

BIRTHSEX OVERRIDES Patient.gender: the join key is COALESCE(us_core_birthsex, gender), so whenever the US Core birthsex extension is present it wins for ALL THREE gender columns; Patient.gender is used only as the fallback. The view (mapspec/views/Patient__person.view.json) extracts birthsex via extension(%usCoreBirthsex).valueCode.first().

cm.gender_to_omop is flattened from TWO groups, both verified against the live table:
  source_code | concept_id | source_concept_id | (group)
  ------------+------------+-------------------+---------------------------
  M           |       8507 |              8507 | v3-AdministrativeGender (birthsex)
  F           |       8532 |              8532 | v3-AdministrativeGender (birthsex)
  OTH         |       8521 |                 0 | v3-AdministrativeGender
  UNK         |       8551 |                 0 | v3-AdministrativeGender
  male        |       8507 |                 0 | administrative-gender (Patient.gender)
  female      |       8532 |                 0 | administrative-gender
  other       |       8521 |                 0 | administrative-gender
  unknown     |       8551 |                 0 | administrative-gender

WHY source_concept_id differs by case: the v3-AdministrativeGender group declares the `omop-source-vocabulary='Gender'` extension, so the materializer looks each source_code up in vocab.concept WHERE vocabulary_id='Gender'. Athena's Gender vocabulary uses single-letter concept_codes (M=8507, F=8532, O=8521, U=8551). So uppercase 'M'/'F' match a real Gender concept_code and carry source_concept_id 8507/8532; 'OTH'/'UNK' do NOT match any concept_code and stay 0. The lowercase administrative-gender group has NO omop-source-vocabulary extension at all, so every Patient.gender value keeps source_concept_id 0. (Verified: vocab.concept 8507 MALE / 8532 FEMALE / 8521 OTHER / 8551 UNKNOWN, all domain_id='Gender'; 8507 & 8532 standard_concept='S', 8521 & 8551 non-standard.)

ABSENT vs UNKNOWN: a literal Patient.gender='unknown' DOES map (cm row → 8551). Only a TRULY ABSENT gender (no birthsex AND no gender) leaves the join key NULL, finds no cm row, and COALESCE(...,0) yields 0 for all three columns. Both behaviors are covered as distinct variants — they are not the same thing.

Birth date is held CONSTANT at '1986-07-04' across every variant so the gender diff is the only thing that moves. EXTRACT(YEAR/MONTH/DAY FROM birth_date::date) → year_of_birth=1986, month_of_birth=7, day_of_birth=4. birth_datetime falls back to birthDate-at-midnight ('1986-07-04T00:00:00') because no patient-birthTime extension is present (Synthea never emits one).

NON-VARYING CONTEXT (held minimal & constant so only gender varies): no us-core-race / us-core-ethnicity / omop-race / omop-ethnicity extensions → race_concept_id=0, ethnicity_concept_id=0, race_source_value=NULL, ethnicity_source_value=NULL, race_source_concept_id=0, ethnicity_source_concept_id=0. No address → location_id=NULL. No generalPractitioner / managingOrganization → provider_id=NULL, care_site_id=NULL. person_source_value = Patient.id (FHIR resource UUID, a traceability anchor — never a PHI identifier).

Every variant emits exactly ONE person row and nothing else.

cases.desc: us-core-birthsex 'M' → gender_concept_id 8507 AND gender_source_concept_id 8507 (uppercase M is an Athena Gender concept_code)
us-core-birthsex 'F' → gender_concept_id 8532 AND gender_source_concept_id 8532 (uppercase F is an Athena Gender concept_code)
birthsex PRESENT overrides Patient.gender: gender=male + birthsex=F → 8532 (F wins via COALESCE(us_core_birthsex, gender))
only Patient.gender 'female', NO birthsex → gender_concept_id 8532 BUT gender_source_concept_id 0 (lowercase administrative-gender group has no omop-source-vocabulary)
Patient.gender 'unknown', no birthsex → gender_concept_id 8551 (cm.gender_to_omop maps 'unknown'→8551; NOT 0), gender_source_concept_id 0
gender ABSENT (no Patient.gender, no birthsex) → join key NULL, no cm row → gender_concept_id 0, gender_source_value NULL, gender_source_concept_id 0
